---

##    8\. God Momentum (Part I): What Is It? 

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/sqhYQkRRVg6GZMwCtIOg_godmomentum-2.PNG)

[In the previous module you learned about Daily Practice.](https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/25245041)

The Daily Practice is 15 minutes to 1 hour a day of something you get better at. Then you go on with your life etc.. until the next day same time, same thing, every day.

Daily Practice is the minimum.

But God Momentum...

God Momentum takes over most of your day and requires energy.

_God Momentum is when you're ready to make art your life._

### "You don't need discipline. When you know what you need to do, you just do it." Karl Lagerfeld

People have it wrong. They think "motivation" can kick them into gear. 

The problem is... Motivation and willpower require a tremendous amount of energy. And because of this most people can't rely on those energies to get things done "day in, day out".

Increasing your momentum is the better way towards success.

Momentum uses EXISTING energy to manufacture MORE of it.

How do you get on that kind of roll?

There are two ways to gain momentum. 

One is by increasing your energy.

Everyone goes down that route.

They'll even try diets and take pills to bio-hack their way to success.

But there's another other way that's much less popular.

And ironically, it's the easiest to do.

If you can put yourself through 30 days of violent daily action. If you can commit to that, you can completely change your future.

Focused relentless daily action gets you to a point where your energy start to feed itself.

The more you act, the more you wanna act. The action is will be it's own reward.

Energy begets energy.

You won't need "discipline" or "willpower". Objects in motion stay in motion.

**The second way of gaining momentum is by reducing friction**

Until you get that “hockey stick”-like momentum.

**Once you have speed that has no friction, you have achieved God Momentum.**

God Momentum is the snowball effect where working hard becomes easy.

_God Momentum is when you've been explosive for so many days in a row that NOT being explosive feels weird. It’s when you no longer question waking up early, or saying "no" to some party this weekend etc... And even natural low energy days when you just don't feel "motivated" don't matter. This powerful force magically guides you to do it anyway.  _

You start dreaming about doing things that other people (and your former self) shy away from.

Synchronicity starts to happen... You find yourself being at the right place at the right time, and getting emails where people want to help you out of the blue... Why? Your energy is infectious.

Confirmation bias starts to happen... Because you've only been engaging in success-driven activities, now the world throws you all sorts of opportunities...

Energy begets energy.

A thing called "The Power Law" goes into effect and you start seeing exponential gains for even the smallest actions.

All that effort you made at the beginning... Now the same effort has compound interest results.

Your process of transformation becomes so beautiful to you that...

You HATE the idea of conflation.

Conflation is when you take something that happens over a long period of time and condense it so that it happens quicker.

Storytellers do it in movies and novels all the time so that the story is less boring.

It's like when they show a couple falling in love in a 20 second montage in a movie

Conflation is good in storytelling... but bad in the real life journey of achieving success.

In real life, progress happens slowly over time and when you start to feel its effects you're going to want to lean into it. 

Even though when you tell the story later you’ll probably make it shorter. But when you experience it you'll want to feel every moment.

**God Momentum is as exhilarating as sex.**

The longer things take, the deeper and more meaningful they become. 

But that doesn’t mean everything will take super long... It just means that everything has its process and you will come to enjoy and respect it.

Remember. That painting may have taken 30 minutes to create, but it’s the 3 days of hitting a wall before it "came pouring out" that made it possible. 

### "Success is a process, not an event." Gary Halbert

Remember the British guys that won the rowing competition at the 2000 Sydney Olympics?

Do you know the story of how they managed to do it even though they were the least likely to win?

Their coach always asked the same question before every decision they had to make:

"Will it make the boat go faster?" 

If a training regiment or a a diet regiment wasn't making the boat go faster it was abandoned. 

They put everything through that filter

Everything was centered on increasing energy.

Is that burger bringing you closer to what you're trying to achieve? Or would you achieve more with a salad that keeps you metabolism running high for a few more hours of cognitive genius. 

**But the secret to God Momentum is reducing friction.**

Is Instagram bringing you closer to what you're trying to achieve? Or did you just lose 3 hours and now feel like shit? Get rid of it

Stop hanging out with negative people. If they're going to be at the dinner, don't go.

Some people (including your own internal bad cop) will tell you that eliminating fun activities isn't really "living life". And that "You have to enjoy yourself sometimes too.

You've been "enjoying" yourself for years. Are you ready to WORK now?

Don't worry. This isn't forever.

You'll be in this mode few (memorable) years so you can live an extraordinary life that only 1% of the world gets to see

If you want that life... Then you have to pay the price that others have paid. And make the sacrifices that others have made.

But what those people saying "You have to live life sometimes" don't know is:

**When you join this club... Of disciplined athletes, of prolific creators, of black excellence, and of women who run with wolves... You get to experience a side of life that is more rewarding than the humdrum life the majority is leading.**

You'll be happy to leave all those ordinary things behind

But a word of warning to you. No matter how many Mystery School modules you read...

And no matter how many inspiring emails you absorb...

_You'll never get God Momentum without those first 21 days of violently focused action. There's no way around it._

That's when everything I've been saying will take on new meaning.

You're standing at the door today. 

And it looks like you've been standing on the porch pacing... Wondering if you should ring the bell?

You should.

[_This is my invitation for you to come in._](https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/21445027)

_LISTEN TO THIS:_

militarymindset.wav

[ Download ](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/u3xlgY5MTpK81TxkNjZ9_militarymindset.wav)

_(_[_Ant Chamberlain_](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pVlz-zScwMk) _, a 21 year old musician explains how if you don't reach your goals it's because you're not ready to adopt the 'military mindset' that's needed)_

I always had the passion. A year ago the passion got way stronger. And since doing the God momentum challenge, the momentum hasn't stopped for one day. I wake up and work on my website, then exercise, then eat a healthy meal, then make art for the rest of my time. I even work on what I can on my work breaks, but I do still have this terrible feeling when I catch myself on youtube reels, or having a long unrelated conversation with someone. I still feel like I don't deserve the label obsessed. But one thing i know for sure, is obsessed and possessed is very much the life style I want, I'm very close and I'll do anything to try any perfect my habits to get there.

"Don't worry. This isn't forever. You'll be in this mode few (memorable) years so you can live an extraordinary life that only 1% of the world gets to see If you want that life... Then you have to pay the price that others have paid. And make the sacrifices that others have made." Here i feel conflicted, because to me what i want to is to enjoy the process, the frustration and the boredom... well maybe enjoy is the wrong word but perhaps "embrace" is more accurate; as to me it feels like setting up a mindset of "i'll start being happy/enjoying life/stress free after x or y" is a honey trap, as true peace of mind is when you are able to improve yourself without anxiety

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/21444354/comments/27315465)

One way to gain speed in the beginning is a sketchbook. I learned this the hard way. Always unsure what to draw and why. Blank papers are scary. I started a sketchbook with the intention to make each page as ugly as possible. Each new line shouldn't make sense. Left handed, blind, to the rhythm of the music, and then intentionally making it ugly. This thought me one thing: you cant be scared of the next move. And: you are way more likely to mess around and try new things and find new things. And one fragment of a page lead to a new style, which lead to a whole new sketchbook filled with rough ideas, sketches, color ideas. And whenever now I want to start, I flick through it and either copy one, or start one of those sketches in big. You never start from 0 with a sketchbook. You always get a kickstart to gain your speed.

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

I had a friend ask me today, like how do I do it? With 3 young kids, a house to take care of and a relationship with my husband, how do I still take courses (like TMS) and keep creating. And I realized it’s because I am obsessed with what I love to do. If you love it, you’ll absolutely make the time. Otherwise, it’s just a hobby.

Goosebumps and tears in my eyes. Shadow identified, God Momentum Schedule and journal set up, website domain bought, and we have only just begun. My desk looks VERY different to what it looked like a week ago. I have indeed spent most of my life enjoying myself, it's time to work. I am here.

![Gavin Wren](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/6e47838e1e80eb168bf3d03a56ce9f2f?d=mm&s=70)

Gavin Wren

Awaiting Review 

4 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/21444354/comments/15260981)

I LOVE the "will it make the boat go faster" philosophy and can 100% relate to it. For a long time I've been grappling with how to apply my successful experiences in physical activities (such as sport and physical work) to create success in intellectual or creative work (photography, writing, research). Physical endurance has always felt far easier than mental endurance, but the Mastery School is really helping with that. The 'boat' philosophy has really helped to focus my creative processes over the last week, thanks so much for writing about it, I love it!

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

LOGY BEAR

Awaiting Review 

5 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/21444354/comments/14900634)

I read a quote from my book by Deepak Chopra. He was talking about someone who had a near death experience, he was deemed dead for two hours and the guy had one things that he took away from his after life experience. He said "People ought to stop trying to become god, and realize that we are already god. And god is becoming us" and that quote just brings that untold feeling. Thought you might like it Bronques <3 Thank you for you're work, your sacrifices, and your patience to be one of the leaders of the this modern day renaissance era, a true father. May the force be with you

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)
